steps.title: Identify what the question requires
steps.content: The example needs a neighboring species with a negative effect value. Two criteria must both be satisfied: (1) the plant appears in the “Neighboring species” column (not only as a target), and (2) its effect value is negative (below zero). Scan the table for a minus sign in the effect-value column next to a plant listed as a neighbor.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — Himalayan balsam.: Himalayan balsam is a neighboring species, but its effect value is +0.7905 — positive, not negative. ✗
  • Choice B — common dandelion.: Common dandelion appears as a neighboring species with an effect value of −0.6254 — the only negative value in the table for a neighboring species. ✓
  • Choice C — star chickweed.: Star chickweed appears only as a target species in the table, not as a neighboring species. ✗
  • Choice D — Virginia spring beauty.: Virginia spring beauty is a neighboring species, but its effect value is +0.4853 — positive, not negative. ✗
That matches Choice B — common dandelion..
